dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for a reported Stroke/CVA at 12 [STREET_ADDRESS] in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Jake, an EMT with the rescue squad. What led you to call 911 today?
My mother has been unresponsive in her bedroom all day. She won't open her eyes or respond.
Does she open her eyes to any stimulus? For example, when I shine a light nearby, does she react?
Hello, can you tell me your name for me?
I'm going to gently touch her shoulder. Does she respond to tactile input in any way?
Her eyes are closed most of the time. There is no significant eye opening observed.
She is making sounds, but the sounds are not in coherent words or sentences.
She moved slightly when touched, with minimal strength and purpose.
She is alert in the sense of minimal eye contact, but orientation cannot be assessed.
Let me check her airway. She appears to be breathing on her own, and her airway is patent.
Respirations are 24 per minute, regular.
Heart rate is 54 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is 180 over 50 in the right arm.
Blood glucose level is 101 mg/dL-within normal limits.
SpO2 was initially 0 but has improved to 91% while on oxygen via NRB.
Skin is warm and dry, but poor turgor is seen in the hands.
EKG monitor reading shows sinus bradycardia and prominent U-waves.
Respirations are normal in rate and depth, with no abnormal chest movement or adventitious breath sounds.
The skin is dry. Pallor is noted in extremities.
Pupils are 2 mm bilaterally. There is a surgical scar on the left frontal skull, consistent with previous shunt placement.
Breath sounds are clear. Chest rises evenly with no signs of pain.
Her abdomen is distended. On palpation, she grimaces but no rigidity is observed.
She is unable to answer questions. Orientation cannot be assessed.
Her breathing rate has increased to 33 breaths per minute.
Heart rate has decreased to 49 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is 190 over 51 now.
SpO2 remains at 91% after O2 administration.
EKG remains consistent with sinus bradycardia and no ST changes.
Let's start an IV. Can we access the midline vein in her left arm?
IV was successfully established in the left arm after two attempts. It is patent.
Initiating 4-lead ECG monitoring per protocol.
4-lead ECG monitoring was initiated.
12-lead ECG is being obtained to assess for any ischemic changes.
A 12-lead ECG has been acquired and shows minimal signs of left ventricular hypertrophy and T wave abnormalities.
Given altered mental status and prior stroke, following protocol medical - altered mental status (protocol 3 - 15).
Let's recheck the vitals. Is the breathing more stable now?
Heart rate is 49 and breathing remains at 27 per minute.
Blood pressure on the left arm is 106 over 32.
IV is patent and ETO2 monitoring is now in use.
Abdomen is still distended with guarding, but no reportable pain.
No recent pain complaints. Has her last oral intake or time of voiding changed recently?
We will take her to [COMPANY_NAME] for further stroke evaluation. Is this her usual hospital?
Can we get permission to transport with IV and ECG monitoring?
Yes, go ahead. I'll prepare her ID and medication list for the bag.
Let's confirm vitals again before transport. SpO2 is currently at 100%.
Blood pressure is 152 over 47 with normal EKG tracing.
IV is patent and EKG shows stable readings. No outward pain observed.
Pain assessment scores are zero. Does she typically express pain?
She remains non-responsive. No specific affect described.
She is now on the stretcher with continuous monitoring applied.
Vitals remain consistent. No emergent interventions are needed at this time.
Continue with vitals and repeat EKG as needed during transport.
Other than her shunt and history of seizures, are there any recent surgeries or injuries?
She shows no sign of pain even with a distended abdomen.
All set. We're heading to [COMPANY_NAME]. Ready for transport?
Transport prepared. Marjorie is set for transfer.
SpO2 is at 100%, BP is 152 over 47, and HR is 49.
IV line is patent and capnography is in use.
Marjorie is being moved from the room. O2 and IV are supported during the move.
No new changes have been noted. Vitals are within expected limits.
A second 12-lead ECG has been obtained and findings remained unchanged.
Abdomen remains distended and guarding evident. No history of pain given.
U-waves still present in EKG. SpO2 was previously noted at 77%.
She is now en route to [COMPANY_NAME] for stroke evaluation.
SpO2 remains at 100%, BP is 152 over 47 and HR is 49 beats per minute.
A surgical shunt is confirmed in the neck as previously noted.
All procedures and monitoring were successful. No changes noted.
She is now en route to [COMPANY_NAME] for stroke evaluation.
Vitals remain consistent. Patient is stable at this time.
IV is patent, oxygen normal and EKG remains stable.
No new findings noted since she was secured for transport.
IV remained patent during transport. No reported issues.
Arrival to [COMPANY_NAME] in 10 minutes is expected. Updates will be sent en route.
Per protocol, vitals will be rechecked every 5 minutes.
4-lead EKG stable throughout with no new patterns.
Marjorie is doing well en route. Pre-hospital report will be sent.
No pain reported or observed. She shows no discomfort or movement.
A surgical shunt is confirmed in the neck as previously noted.
All procedures and monitoring were successful. No changes noted.
Arrival to [COMPANY_NAME] in 10 minutes is expected. Updates will be sent en route.
Vitals remain consistent. Patient is stable at this time.
IV is patent, oxygen normal and EKG remains stable.
No new findings noted since she was secured for transport.
IV remained patent during transport. No reported issues.
All set. We're heading to [COMPANY_NAME]. Ready for transport?
Transport prepared. Marjorie is set for transfer.
SpO2 is at 100%, BP is 152 over 47, and HR is 49.
IV line is patent and capnography is in use.
Marjorie is being moved from the room. O2 and IV are supported during the move.
No new changes have been noted. Vitals are within expected limits.
A second 12-lead ECG has been obtained and findings remained unchanged.
Abdomen remains distended and guarding evident. No history of pain given.
U-waves still present in EKG with no new changes observed.
She is now en route to [COMPANY_NAME] for stroke evaluation.
SpO2 remains at 100%, BP is 152 over 47 and HR is 49 beats per minute.
A surgical shunt is confirmed in the neck as previously noted.
All procedures and monitoring were successful. No changes noted.
She is now en route to [COMPANY_NAME] for stroke evaluation.
Vitals remain consistent. Patient is stable at this time.
